Train's coming, gonna lay on the track
 A happy ride here, layin' on my back
 Let the train wheel come and take my life
 Yeah, let the big steel make it alright

 Because I've got no friend to tell the trial
 Lots of enemies so quick to defile
 I suppose I'm shrunk again but I don't care
 All I know is we're less than the air
 So let's not compare

 Feeling sacrificed, you?
 Are you feeling slammed?
 And are you claustrophobic on this open land?
 Or feeling crumpled up and dented in?
 And are you trapped within your very own skin?
 Well, it's like a jail, you, you can't see the bars
 You can't escape it by bleeding through your scars
 Self- deprecation is a boring hand
 And you're just bringin' everybody down, man

 Feeling sorry, are ya?
 Feeling blue, and are ya?
 Down and out, now and overdue
 Feeling smothered by the deep blue sky
 Does the sunset hurt your eye?
 Oh, well, you're not the only one who
 Who got caught in the rain
 This whole world don't revolve around your pain
 And you can go on, yeah, and spin out in misery
 But I'll take the sun and the sea
 And oh, sham- sham- a- ling- o
 And oh, dip- de- dip
 My mental record starts to skip
 An orange, a lemon, lime, and oh, a tangelo
 On the coconut tree next to the mighty mango

 Oh, the high Sierra, yeah
 In my rearview mirror
 Ain't it beauty, beautiful, my lovely dear?
 We got time, so, so let's not diffuse
 Let's wear holes in our shoes
